column6: «~Cantāte nōbīs dē canticīs Siōnis.~»^ for there they asked us
column9: and they that carried us away
column11: "Sing ye to us of the songs of Sion."

1 ^ Super flūmina Babilōnis: 4 ^ ¿~Quōmodō cantābimus canticum Dominī in terrā aliēnā~? ^ How shall we sing the song of the Lord in a strange land~?

quum recordārēmur Siōnem. ^ Upon the ri: oblīviōnī dētur dextera mea. ^ If I forget thee
let my right hand be forgotten.

illīc sēdimus et flēvimus: sī nōn meminerō tuī~; sī nōn prōposuerō Jerozolimam in principiō laetitiae meae. ^ Let my tongue cleave to my jaws
quum recordārēmur Siōnem. ^ Upon the ri: if I do not remember thee~; if I make not Jerusalem the beginning of my joy.

illīc sēdimus et flēvimus: Domine fīliōrum Edōmis in diē Jerozolimae
exinānīte usque ad fundāmentum in eā.~»^ Remember
column7: the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem

quum recordārēmur Siōnem. ^ Upon the ri: quam retribuistī nōbīs. ^ O miserable daughter of Babylon~; blessed shall he be who shall repay thee thy payment which thou hast paid us.

illīc sēdimus et flēvimus: et allīdet parvulōs tuōs ad petram. ^ Blessed be he that shall take and dash thy little ones against the rock.
